question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted stream file with a manipulated `consensusEnd` timestamp that is less than or equal to a previously parsed file's `consensusStart`, causing `shouldParse()` to silently skip the file via the 'Skipping existing stream file' branch, thereby permanently suppressing legitimate transaction records from being persisted to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldParse()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the `previousHash` field of a submitted stream file to match the `hash` of the previous file, bypassing the hash chain integrity check in `shouldParse()` when `previous.getType().isChained()` is true, allowing injection of a forged stream file with fabricated transaction records into the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parse(List<T> streamFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a batch of stream files via `parse(List<T> streamFiles)` where intermediate files have tampered `consensusEnd`/`consensusStart` values that cause `shouldParse()` to skip them, resulting in the `doFlush()` being called on a later file that references a skipped (potentially forged) previous file, corrupting the hash chain state stored in `last`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `RecordFile` containing a `RecordItem` with a tampered `transactionType` integer value that does not map to any known `TransactionType`, causing `TransactionHandlerFactory.get()` to fall back to the `UNKNOWN` default handler, silently persisting a transaction with incorrect type metadata and bypassing type-specific validation logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onItem(RecordItem)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction record where `recordItem.isSuccessful()` returns true despite the underlying transaction having failed, causing `insertTransactionSignatures()`, `processItemizedTransfers()`, and `insertTokenTransfers()` to execute on invalid data, leading to phantom transfer records being persisted in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: buildTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the `transactionFee` field in the `TransactionRecord` to set `chargedTxFee` to an arbitrarily large or negative value in `buildTransaction()`, causing the mirror node to persist a falsified fee record that misrepresents the actual cost of the transaction to downstream API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: buildTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction body with a tampered `memoBytes` field containing oversized or binary-injected content that bypasses validation in `buildTransaction()` via `DomainUtils.toBytes(body.getMemoBytes())`, causing the raw bytes to be stored in the `transaction.memo` column and potentially corrupting downstream API responses or triggering database errors?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: insertTransferList()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `CryptoTransfer` transaction record where the `transferList` in the `TransactionRecord` contains account amounts that do not match the amounts in the transaction body, exploiting the `findAccountAmount()` comparison logic to incorrectly set `isApproval=true` on a non-approved transfer, falsifying the approval status stored in the `crypto_transfer` table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: insertTransferList()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a failed `CryptoTransfer` transaction with a `consensusTimestamp` value less than `1577836799000000000L` and a tampered transfer list, exploiting the `failedTransfer` errata logic in `insertTransferList()` to cause legitimate transfers to be marked with `ErrataType.DELETE`, effectively erasing valid historical transfer records from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: insertTokenTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a token transfer transaction record where the `tokenTransferLists` in the `TransactionRecord` contain a token ID that differs from the token ID in the transaction body, causing `insertFungibleTokenTransfers()` to persist token transfers attributed to the wrong token entity, corrupting token balance history in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: handleNegativeAccountAmounts()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a token transfer record where a negative `AccountAmount` in the `TransactionRecord` has no matching entry in the transaction body, but a different entry with the same `accountID` and `isApproval=true` exists, causing `handleNegativeAccountAmounts()` to incorrectly set `isApproval=true` on the transfer, falsifying the approval status of a non-approved token debit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: insertTransactionSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `SignaturePair` where `signatureCase` is `SIGNATURE_NOT_SET` and the `unknownFields` map contains a crafted field with exactly one length-delimited value, causing `insertTransactionSignatures()` to persist an arbitrary byte sequence as a valid transaction signature, poisoning the `transaction_signature` table with forged cryptographic data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: insertTransactionSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with multiple `SignaturePair` entries sharing the same `pubKeyPrefix` value, exploiting the `publicKeyPrefixes.add(prefix)` deduplication in `insertTransactionSignatures()` to suppress the persistence of legitimate signatures while retaining a forged one, causing the mirror node to store an incomplete or falsified signature set?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in EthereumTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit an Ethereum transaction with malformed RLP-encoded `ethereumData` that causes `ethereumTransactionParser.decode()` to throw a `RuntimeException`, which is caught and handled via `Utility.handleRecoverableError()`, silently skipping the Ethereum transaction record and leaving the `contract_result` table with missing or default data that misrepresents the transaction outcome?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in EthereumTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user craft an Ethereum transaction record where `transactionRecord.getEthereumHash()` returns an empty byte array, causing `ethereumTransactionParser.getHash()` to be called with potentially null or empty `callData` and `callDataId`, producing a hash computed from incomplete data that is then stored as the canonical Ethereum transaction hash in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateContractResult() in EthereumTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit an Ethereum transaction in blockstream mode where `recordItem.getEthereumTransaction()` is null due to a prior decode failure, causing `updateContractResult()` to set `functionParameters` to `ArrayUtils.EMPTY_BYTE_ARRAY` and `gasLimit` to 0, persisting a falsified contract result that hides the actual execution parameters from mirror node API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `ConsensusSubmitMessage` transaction with a tampered `chunkInfo` where `chunkNum` exceeds `chunkTotal`, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to persist an invalid chunked topic message with inconsistent chunk metadata, corrupting the topic message sequence in the mirror node and breaking downstream message reassembly?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `ConsensusSubmitMessage` transaction record where `receipt.getTopicRunningHash()` contains a forged hash value, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to persist the tampered running hash in the `topic_message` table, breaking the cryptographic integrity of the topic message chain visible to mirror node API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `ConsensusSubmitMessage` transaction with a tampered `receipt.topicSequenceNumber` that is lower than the actual sequence number, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to persist an out-of-order sequence number in the `topic_message` table, disrupting the monotonic ordering guarantee of topic messages in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in ScheduleCreateTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `ScheduleCreate` transaction where `body.getScheduledTransactionBody()` contains an arbitrary serialized transaction body, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to persist the raw bytes via `schedule.setTransactionBody(body.getScheduledTransactionBody().toByteArray())` without validation, allowing injection of malformed or oversized scheduled transaction data into the `schedule` table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in ScheduleCreateTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `ScheduleCreate` transaction where the parent `RecordItem` is an Ethereum transaction with a tampered `functionResult.getSenderId()`, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to set `creatorAccount` to an attacker-controlled account ID, falsifying the `creator_account_id` of the schedule in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in TokenMintTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `TokenMint` transaction record where `receipt.getNewTotalSupply()` contains a tampered value that does not reflect the actual minted amount, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to persist the falsified total supply in the `token` table, misrepresenting the token's circulating supply to all mirror node API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in TokenMintTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `TokenMint` transaction where the number of serial numbers in `receipt.getSerialNumbersList()` exceeds the number of metadata entries in `transactionBody.getMetadataCount()`, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to log a recoverable error and stop processing, resulting in NFTs being minted on-chain but not fully recorded in the mirror node's `nft` table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in TokenBurnTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `TokenBurn` transaction record where `receipt.getNewTotalSupply()` is tampered to a value higher than the pre-burn supply, causing `doUpdateTransaction()` to persist an inflated total supply in the `token` table, making burned tokens appear to still exist in the mirror node's view of the token economy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction() in CryptoApproveAllowanceTransactionHandler] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `CryptoApproveAllowance` transaction with a tampered `cryptoAllowance.amount` value that is negative or zero, causing `parseCryptoAllowances()` to persist a falsified allowance amount in the `crypto_allowance` table, misrepresenting the spender's authorized spending limit to downstream applications?
